betCard.title: Round 7 Charity Bet
betCard.kickoff: Review after team lists on Tuesday 14 Apr 2026
why: The betting record is clean at 2-0, so there is no need to force a play early.
Round 6 reminded us that form shifts can hit hard when favourites are overrated.
Holding until official ins and outs land should keep the process sharper.
risks: Jumping too early means guessing through injury noise instead of using it.
Market movement can erase value if we lock in before the real picture is clear.
Round 7 has several reset games where public sentiment may overreact to Round 6.

charity.title: Mark Hughes Foundation Ledger
charity.summary: All profits are earmarked for the Mark Hughes Foundation.
notes: Round 5: Newcastle head to head at $2.30 won and collected $11.50.
Round 6: North Queensland Cowboys head to head at $2.00 won and collected $10.00.

watchlist: Tigers have turned surprise energy into a real ladder push.
Warriors just made a loud statement against Melbourne.
Storm and Eels both need to earn trust back next week.
Dragons keep sliding and are now firmly in pressure territory.

sections.title: Situation Report
sections.heading: Round 7 opens with the board resetting, not chasing
lines: As of Monday, 13 April 2026, Round 7 is a setup week rather than a locking week because official team lists are still pending.
The board comes in off a 4/8 tipping round, but the charity process stayed clean thanks to the Cowboys win and a 2-0 betting record.
The key lesson is to carry forward the form upgrades for Tigers and Warriors while resisting the urge to overcorrect on every upset.

sections.title: Bet Analysis
sections.heading: Round 7 charity play stays uncommitted for now
lines: There is no value in pretending a Round 7 play is locked before official team lists and injury confirmations are out.
The advantage right now is patience: let the market and late availability settle, then attack the clearest edge.
With the charity ledger already at $11.50 profit, discipline matters more than action.

sections.title: Form Radar
sections.heading: Who deserves the Round 7 upgrade or downgrade
lines: Hot: Tigers, Warriors, Cowboys, Bulldogs, Sea Eagles.
Melbourne and Parramatta have both moved from trust teams to prove-it teams.

sections.title: Intel Notes
sections.heading: The data points most worth checking before locking Round 7
lines: Broncos, Cowboys, Tigers, Dragons, Rabbitohs and Warriors all carry injury or availability angles worth re-checking once team lists drop.
The casualty ward now has fresh concerns around Jye Gray, Samuela Fainu, Tyson Frizell, Cory Paix and several Cowboys and Eels bodies.
Do not lock any charity play until the Tuesday 14 April 2026 team-list release and any immediate market reaction are both visible.

footerNotes: Built from the attached Claude prototype and reorganised into a maintainable Codex workflow.
